# Beudox HR - Technical Context for AI Assistants

## Architecture Overview

Beudox HR is a single-page application (SPA) built with React and TypeScript, using Vite as the build tool. The application follows a component-based architecture with clear separation of concerns:

- **Frontend**: React 18 with TypeScript for type safety
- **Backend**: Supabase (PostgreSQL with real-time subscriptions)
- **State Management**: React Query for server state, React hooks for local state
- **Routing**: React Router with protected routes and role-based access control
- **UI**: ShadCN UI component library built on Radix UI primitives and Tailwind CSS
- **Forms**: React Hook Form with Zod schema validation
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS with custom CSS variables for theming

## Key Architectural Decisions

### Authentication Flow
- Uses Supabase Auth with JWT tokens
- Mandatory password change on first login
- Role-based access control with route protection
- Session persistence with automatic refresh

### Data Flow
- React Query handles all API calls to Supabase
- Optimistic updates for better UX
- Real-time subscriptions for live data (notifications, updates)
- Caching strategies to minimize API calls

### Component Structure
- Atomic design principles with reusable UI components
- Page components for routes
- Feature-specific component folders
- Custom hooks for business logic separation

### Database Design
- PostgreSQL via Supabase with Row Level Security (RLS)
- Comprehensive schema for HR entities (employees, attendance, payroll, etc.)
- Migrations for schema evolution
- Edge functions for complex business logic

## Key Files and Their Purposes

### Core Application Files
- `src/App.tsx`: Main application component with routing setup
- `src/main.tsx`: Application entry point
- `src/index.css`: Global styles and CSS variables

### Authentication & Authorization
- `src/hooks/useAuth.tsx`: Authentication state management
- `src/lib/role-access.ts`: Role-based permission checking
- `src/components/MandatoryPasswordChange.tsx`: Password change modal

### Layout & Navigation
- `src/components/layout/AppLayout.tsx`: Main application layout
- `src/components/layout/AppSidebar.tsx`: Navigation sidebar
- `src/components/NavLink.tsx`: Custom navigation link component

### Business Logic Components
- `src/components/attendance/AttendanceSummary.tsx`: Complex attendance analytics
- `src/components/SearchableEmployeeSelect.tsx`: Employee selection component
- `src/components/BeudoxLogo.tsx`: Logo component with variants

### Utility Libraries
- `src/lib/utils.ts`: General utility functions (cn for class merging)
- `src/lib/attendance-format.ts`: Attendance data formatting
- `src/lib/role-access.ts`: Access control utilities
- `src/lib/client-activity.ts`: Activity logging and categorization

### Configuration Files
- `vite.config.ts`: Vite configuration with path aliases
- `tailwind.config.ts`: Tailwind CSS configuration
- `tsconfig.json`: TypeScript configuration
- `package.json`: Dependencies and scripts

## Business Logic and Data Flow

### Employee Management
- Employee CRUD operations with profile management
- Role assignments and permission management
- Document storage and retrieval

### Attendance System
- Check-in/check-out recording
- Overtime calculation (regular and holiday)
- Absence tracking and reporting
- Analytics with working days, attendance rates, and anomalies

### Payroll Processing
- Salary calculations with allowances and deductions
- Payslip generation
- Overtime compensation
- Tax and benefit calculations

### Leave Management
- Leave request workflow (apply → approve/reject)
- Leave balance tracking
- Different leave types (vacation, sick, etc.)
- Calendar integration

### Evaluation System
- Performance reviews with customizable parameters
- Regular and ad-hoc evaluations
- Rating systems and feedback collection

### Project Management
- Project creation and team assignment
- Task tracking and progress monitoring
- Client association and billing

## Database Schema Highlights

The application uses a comprehensive PostgreSQL schema with tables for:
- `employees`: Core employee data with auth integration
- `attendance_records`: Time tracking data
- `leave_requests`: Leave management
- `payroll_records`: Salary and compensation data
- `projects`: Project management
- `evaluations`: Performance reviews
- `companies`: Multi-tenant support
- `public_holidays`: Holiday configuration

## API Patterns

- RESTful endpoints via Supabase client
- Real-time subscriptions for live updates
- File uploads for documents and images
- PDF generation for reports and invoices
- Email notifications via Supabase Edge Functions

## Development Workflow

- Component tagging for development (lovable-tagger)
- ESLint for code quality
- Vitest for unit testing
- Playwright for E2E testing
- Hot module replacement for development

## Security Considerations

- Row Level Security (RLS) on all database tables
- JWT-based authentication
- Input validation with Zod schemas
- XSS protection via React's built-in sanitization
- CSRF protection through SameSite cookies

## Performance Optimizations

- Code splitting with Vite
- Lazy loading of routes
- React Query caching
- Image optimization and lazy loading
- Bundle analysis and tree shaking

## Deployment

The application is designed to be deployed as a static site to platforms like Vercel, Netlify, or Supabase hosting, with the Supabase backend providing all server-side functionality.
